opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There is a severe, constant fire burning in the pit of my stomach, and I feel completely full after taking just two bites of toast.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The burning has been unrelenting for two days and I have basically stopped eating because I fill up straight away — I feel weak and worn out and the antacids aren't touching it.

symptom_narrative.onset: a low-grade fullness after meals started about four weeks ago; the severe burning flared up over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: 4 weeks of fullness, 48 hours of severe burning
symptom_narrative.character: a constant burning fire in the upper middle of the stomach, and feeling stuffed full after only a couple of mouthfuls
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the burning is severe and never really lets up; the fullness means I can't finish a normal meal
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: high up in the middle of my stomach, just below the breastbone
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the fullness is worst after eating; the burning is there most of the time and isn't clearly tied to meals
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much — I have tried several bottles of over-the-counter antacids and they haven't helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: eating anything more than a couple of bites makes the fullness unbearable
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a bit of nausea, lots of burping, and my upper belly feels bloated; going to the toilet doesn't relieve it
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have never had it this bad before; my appetite was completely normal until this started
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I have been stuck in the armchair, too weak and sore to do my normal jobs around the house, and I am barely eating or drinking

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing knee pain (wear-and-tear arthritis) that I take anti-inflammatories for
past_medical_history.how_managed: daily ibuprofen for the knee, though I stopped it two days ago because of the stomach pain

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: one daily, but stopped in the last 48 hours

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse, who is present and worried about how little the patient has eaten or drunk over the past two days

objective_data_offered.value: about 65 kg on our home scales
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported from a home scale; not a clinic-measured weight

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says the patient looks tired and uncomfortable and keeps pressing a hand against the upper belly
has managed only sips of water and a couple of bites of toast in the last two days
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self/caregiver report of reduced intake — dehydration risk cannot be objectively confirmed over chat
